response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js/DLang expertise and solid software craftsmanship practices, but he is a fundamentally poor match for this role. His C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, falling dramatically short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no discernible fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no evidence of Windows-platform development. His financial-adjacent exposure (RIDE Capital) was an investment-platform startup using Node/TypeScript/GraphQL, not capital markets infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied in production environments
Experience at RIDE Capital provides some financial-services domain exposure, however tangential
Broad polyglot background (DLang, Java, C#, JavaScript) shows ability to adapt to new languages and environments
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professionally), nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years required
Zero fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL is listed as a generic skill with no depth evidence on enterprise RDBMS
